name: NeMo Curator
tagline: GPU-accelerated pipelines to clean, dedupe, and curate training data at scale
seoTitle: NeMo Curator: GPU Data Curation for LLM Training
metaDescription: NeMo Curator is NVIDIA's toolkit for building repeatable, GPU-accelerated pipelines to load, filter, deduplicate, and transform text, image, video, and audio data.
overview: NeMo Curator is an NVIDIA toolkit that helps ML engineers and data teams build repeatable data pipelines for AI training. It loads, filters, deduplicates, and transforms large datasets across four modalities: text, image, video, and audio. The same pipeline can run on a single laptop or scale out across a multi-node Ray cluster.

It is aimed at teams who need production-grade curation rather than one-off notebooks or ad-hoc scripts. It leans on GPU and distributed execution for data-heavy stages like deduplication, classification, embedding, and inference, using NVIDIA RAPIDS (cuDF, cuML, cuGraph) together with Ray.

As a synthetic-data and labeling tool, it provides modality-aware building blocks plus recipes that map to NVIDIA training workflows such as Nemotron and Nemotron-CC, including LLM-based synthetic data generation.
features: Four modalities in one framework: text, image, video, and audio curation
Text operations include deduplication, classification, quality filtering, and language detection
GPU and multi-node execution via NVIDIA RAPIDS (cuDF, cuML, cuGraph) and Ray
Image operations such as aesthetic filtering, NSFW detection, embedding generation, and deduplication
Video and audio pipelines for scene detection, clip extraction, ASR transcription, and WER filtering
Recipes that reproduce NVIDIA training workflows like Nemotron and Nemotron-CC

gettingStarted.intro: NeMo Curator uses the uv package manager. Install uv once, then pick the path that matches your hardware. The CPU smoke test below needs no GPU.

steps.heading: Install uv
steps.body: NeMo Curator uses uv for installation. Install it once on your machine.

steps.heading: CPU smoke test (no GPU required)
steps.body: Create a virtual environment, install the CPU text extra, and verify the import.
steps.code:
uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install "nemo-curator[text_cpu]"
python -c "import nemo_curator; print(nemo_curator.__version__)"

steps.heading: GPU text pipeline (CUDA 12, Linux)
steps.body: On a supported Linux box with a CUDA 12 driver, install the CUDA extra and run the bundled quickstart, which starts Ray and runs a GPU sentiment classification pipeline.
steps.code:
uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install "nemo-curator[text_cuda12]"
python tutorials/quickstart.py

steps.heading: Docker for video and audio
steps.body: Video and audio pipelines need system codec libraries, so the published NGC container is recommended. See the Installation Guide for setup.

useCases: Curating large web datasets (quality filtering, deduplication, domain classification) to pre-train an LLM
Scaling fuzzy and exact deduplication across multiple GPUs instead of slow CPU-based jobs
Reproducing NVIDIA Nemotron-CC style pipelines, including LLM-based synthetic data generation
Building image, video, or audio training sets with modality-specific filtering and embedding steps
